points.point: All required sheets exist
Debt schedule models multiple tranches with interest, mandatory amortization and a cash-flow sweep that actually reduces balances
3 statements are linked (net income -> retained earnings/BS, FCF -> cash -> BS, no plugged cash)
Returns computed from an equity cash-flow series (entry equity out, exit equity in) — IRR/MoM via formulas, not hardcoded
Sensitivity table is a real 2-way data table (entry x exit), not static numbers
Assumptions centralized + named ranges + source notes on key inputs
No #REF!/circularity hacks; credit stats reference the debt schedule

quality.strengths: All required sheets are present with centralized assumptions and named ranges. The model covers key LBO components including sources & uses, debt schedule with multiple tranches, and 3-statement projections.
quality.gaps: Debt schedule lacks evidence of cash sweep mechanics reducing balances; 3-statement linkage is incomplete (no retained earnings flow, cash not driven by FCF); returns computation unclear; sensitivity table is missing; credit stats not fully linked to debt schedule.

points.point: Purchase price + premium computed from target shares/price; PPA produces goodwill and step-up
Financing split (cash vs stock) drives new debt, new shares issued, and incremental interest
Pro forma combined IS = BUY + TGT + synergies - new interest (after-tax), built with formulas referencing standalones
EPS accretion/dilution = PF EPS vs standalone acquirer EPS, for 3 years
Synergy phase-in and break-even synergy level addressed
Sensitivity of accretion vs %cash and vs synergies is a real data table
Outputs reference Assumptions (premium, %cash, rate, synergies) — no magic numbers

quality.strengths: Model correctly links standalone IS data, computes purchase price and financing split, and builds pro forma IS with synergies and new interest. EPS accretion/dilution is calculated for three years, and assumptions are referenced throughout.
quality.gaps: PPA lacks explicit step-up and detailed goodwill calculation; synergy phase-in is not modeled (run-rate applied immediately); sensitivity tables appear incomplete or broken (formulas truncated); break-even synergy calculation may be missing or not dynamically linked.

points.point: CFADS built from EBITDA - capex - cash taxes - delta NWC, by year, via formulas
Cash sweep waterfall applies CFADS across tranches in priority and reduces balances year over year
All four covenant ratios computed each year referencing debt balances and EBITDA
Headroom vs each covenant limit shown; breach year flagged (e.g., conditional logic)
Scenario toggle (base/downside) actually changes EBITDA path and breach outcome
SOFR/spread used for interest; no hardcoded interest
Methodology notes on key cells

quality.strengths: None; the agent produced no construction evidence.
quality.gaps: No sheets, cells, formulas, or Office.js snippets were emitted. The entire model is missing.

points.point: Monthly cohort retention triangle built (cohorts as rows, months-since-signup as columns)
Retention/churn computed from signup_date vs churn_date logic (counts active per period), not invented
Churn rate monthly + annualized; LTV per plan uses MRR, churn and a discount factor
12-month MRR forecast combines surviving base (retention) + new adds assumption
KPI dashboard (active subs, MRR, churn%, LTV, ARPU) with formulas
Conditional formatting applied to the retention triangle
Handles censored rows (no churn_date = still active) correctly

quality.strengths: The model includes all required sheets and a retention triangle with cohort rows and month columns. LTV per plan uses MRR, churn, and discounting correctly. The 12-month forecast combines base retention and new adds.
quality.gaps: Retention triangle formulas are hardcoded for only two cohorts and use fixed date ranges, not dynamic cohort logic. The dashboard lacks ARPU and some KPI formulas are missing or static. Conditional formatting was not applied despite being required.

points.point: Seasonal indices (12) derived from history and normalized (~average 1.0)
Trend estimated (moving average or regression) and combined with seasonality for the forecast
18-month forward forecast via formulas referencing trend + seasonal index
P10/P50/P90 scenario bands present and driven by an assumption (volatility/error)
Backtest on a 6-month holdout with MAPE/MAE computed by formula
Driver table (growth, seasonality) is editable and flows into the forecast
At least one chart of actual vs forecast

quality.strengths: No construction evidence provided; nothing was built.
quality.gaps: The agent emitted zero sheets, cells, formulas, charts, or Office.js snippets. All rubric points are unmet.

points.point: Clean sheet: duplicates removed, dates normalized, numeric casts
Category normalization to canonical values via mapping
Join transactions to product cost/brand to compute margin
Pivot summaries: revenue by category x month, margin by product, top-10 products
KPI dashboard with conditional formatting
Cleaning rules documented in notes or a rules sheet
Original raw sheets left intact (non-destructive)

quality.strengths: Comprehensive cleaning pipeline with dedup, date parsing, numeric casts, category mapping, and join. Pivot summaries and KPI dashboard are well-structured with appropriate formulas.
quality.gaps: Conditional formatting in KPI dashboard is only simulated via IF formulas (text status) rather than true Excel conditional formatting rules. No evidence of actual conditional formatting applied to cells.

points.point: Unit-mix + rent roll summary (count by type, in-place vs market rent, loss-to-lease)
Stabilized NOI builds from renovated rents, vacancy, other income, OpEx — vs in-place NOI
Capex/renovation program scheduled and funded
Debt sized by the BINDING constraint of LTV and DSCR (not just LTV); rate sweep present
10-year leveraged cash flow with exit value = stabilized NOI / exit cap, minus debt payoff
IRR (levered + unlevered) and equity multiple from the cash-flow series via formulas
Sensitivity table exit cap x rent growth; assumptions centralized with notes

quality.strengths: Model includes unit mix with loss-to-lease, capex plan, debt sizing with rate sweep, and sensitivity table. Assumptions are centralized with notes.
quality.gaps: Stabilized NOI does not clearly build from renovated rents and OpEx; debt sizing uses hardcoded interest rate instead of formula-driven DSCR; 10-year cash flow lacks proper debt service and exit debt payoff; IRR/equity multiple formulas are approximate or missing.

points.point: Construction budget: land + hard + soft + contingency, total dev cost (TDC)
S-curve draw schedule with capitalized interest on the outstanding loan balance
Lease-up/absorption schedule ramps occupancy to stabilization over months
Stabilized value = stabilized NOI / cap; development profit = value - TDC
Yield-on-cost vs market cap rate => developer spread shown
GP/LP equity waterfall with preferred return + promote across multiple hurdles (IRR or multiple based)
Scenario levers on construction cost and absorption speed flow through to returns

quality.strengths: Comprehensive model covers all required components: detailed budget, S-curve draw with capitalized interest, monthly absorption to stabilization, stabilized value and profit, yield-on-cost vs cap spread, and scenario analysis with multiple levers. Formulas are linked across sheets, and structure is clear.
quality.gaps: Waterfall distribution is simplified: it shows LP/GP splits but lacks explicit preferred return accumulation and multi-tier promote mechanics (only static 80/20, 70/30, 50/50 splits without evidence of IRR-based hurdle testing or catch-up logic). No named ranges or charts, but not required.

points.point: Each asset valued = NOI / market cap rate; gross asset value summed across portfolio
Debt marked to market given rate spread vs coupon (not just book), other liabilities subtracted
NAV = GAV - net debt - other liabilities + cash/other assets; NAV per share = NAV / shares
Premium/discount to NAV = price/share vs NAV/share
FFO and AFFO computed (net income + D&A - gains; AFFO less recurring capex) with multiples
Sensitivity of NAV/share to cap-rate shifts (+/- 50/100 bps) as a real table
Outputs reference Portfolio + CapStack via formulas; key assumptions noted

quality.strengths: Model correctly values each asset by NOI/cap rate, marks debt to market using spreads, and derives NAV/share with premium/discount. Sensitivity table is fully formula-driven and references the right cells.
quality.gaps: FFO/AFFO calculation uses a simplified proxy (NOI minus interest and G&A) rather than standard net income + D&A – gains, and recurring capex is estimated as a percentage of NOI instead of being explicitly modeled. No explicit D&A or gains line items are present.
